title: Tender Broiled Venezuelan Tomato Empanadas
description: Buttery and springy empanadas filled with tomato puree, perfect for a solo dinner, served on a talavera plate. This shojin-friendly dish is a delicious and satisfying meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Add cold butter to the dry ingredients and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add water to the mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place 1/4 cup of tomato puree in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half circle and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and broil in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 12 minutes, or until golden brown.
